# NeoPolaris AI

> AI strategy, governance, custom development, and cybersecurity consultancy based in Nairobi, Kenya, helping African businesses adopt AI that is safe, legal, and built to last.

NeoPolaris AI works directly with CEOs and CTOs to find where AI creates real, measurable value inside their business, build the systems to capture it, and put the governance and security guardrails in place from day one. Engagements run end-to-end under one team — strategy, governance, development, and security — rather than being split across vendors, and the company trains client teams to own what's built rather than staying dependent on NeoPolaris after delivery. NeoPolaris AI is also building Meridian, an AI operating system giving organisations a unified layer for deploying, monitoring, and governing AI across their operations, currently in early development.

## Site

- [Services](https://neopolaris.ai/services/): The four service lines — AI Strategy, AI Governance, Custom AI Development, and Cybersecurity — and the four-stage engagement process: Discovery, Design, Build & Deploy, Handover & Training.
- [About](https://neopolaris.ai/about/): Background on NeoPolaris AI and why it's built specifically for the African operating environment rather than adapted from a Western consultancy playbook.
- [Meridian](https://neopolaris.ai/meridian/): Product page for Meridian, NeoPolaris AI's in-development AI operating system, with early-access signup.
- [Contact](https://neopolaris.ai/contact/): Enquiry form (name, company, job title, email, service of interest, message) plus direct email, phone/WhatsApp, and location.

## Policies

- [Privacy Policy](https://neopolaris.ai/privacy/): What data NeoPolaris AI collects through this website and client engagements, how it's used, who processes it, and data subject rights under Kenya's Data Protection Act, 2019.
- [Terms of Service](https://neopolaris.ai/terms/): Terms governing use of this website and the baseline for client engagements. Signed service agreements take precedence over these terms where the two conflict.
